sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: AdvisorShares Hotel ETF
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed profile of the AdvisorShares Hotel ETF (BEDZ), an actively managed ETF focusing on consumer discretionary equities, specifically hotels, resorts, and cruise lines. It outlines key financial metrics, performance data, holdings, expenses, and ESG scores. The ETF has an expense ratio of 0.99% and holds major companies like Expedia, Marriott, and Hilton.

feed.title: Kyowa Kirin Co., Ltd. And Kura Oncology, Inc. Advance Ziftomenib With Japan Phase 2 Trial In NPM1 Mutated AML
feed.summary: Kyowa Kirin Co., Ltd. and Kura Oncology, Inc. have initiated a Phase 2 registrational clinical trial in Japan for ziftomenib, an oral menin inhibitor aimed at treating relapsed or refractory NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia (AML). This step is crucial for potential regulatory approval in Japan, following its FDA approval in November 2025 for similar patient populations in the U.S. The trial seeks to confirm the drug's efficacy and safety, addressing a significant unmet medical need for AML patients.

feed.title: Mizuho lowers First Solar stock price target on valuation By Investing.com
feed.summary: Mizuho has lowered its price target for First Solar Inc. (NASDAQ:FSLR) to $243 from $271, while maintaining an "Outperform" rating, citing valuation concerns despite identifying it as a top pick among solar companies due to its domestic manufacturing and earnings visibility. The firm also highlighted Shoals Technologies as a strong second choice, while deeming other solar companies as fairly valued due to execution risk and policy sensitivity. Recent analyst updates and international trade developments, including new antidumping duties and potential Chinese export restrictions, are influencing the broader solar industry outlook.

feed.title: Infosys Shares Rise After Three Sessions Of Decline
feed.summary: Infosys shares saw a 2% rise to Rs 1,178 on the NSE, breaking a three-session decline that included its worst weekly sell-off in six years. Despite investor sentiment being impacted by the company's refusal to share AI-revenue numbers, analysts maintain a largely positive outlook with an average 12-month target price implying a 26% upside.

feed.title: KBR wins $200M transportation tech contract recompete
feed.summary: KBR (NYSE:KBR) has secured a $200 million recompete contract from the U.S. Department of Transportation's Volpe National Transportation Systems Center. Despite KBR shares trading near their 52-week low, InvestingPro analysis suggests the company is undervalued.

feed.title: Domino’s Pizza stock falls premarket as quarterly results miss estimates
feed.summary: Domino's Pizza (NASDAQ: DPZ) reported first-quarter results that fell short of Wall Street expectations, with adjusted earnings per share of $4.13 against an estimated $4.28, and revenue of $1.15 billion against an estimated $1.17 billion. Despite positive U.S. same-store sales growth, international sales declined, and shares fell 3.8% following the announcement. The company announced a $1.0 billion share repurchase program and a quarterly dividend of $1.99 per share.

feed.title: Global Food & Beverage Disinfection & Cleaning Market Outlook 2025-2035 | North America & Europe Expansion Driven by 3M Company, BASF SE
feed.summary: The global food and beverage industrial disinfection and cleaning market is projected to grow from USD 3.6 billion in 2025 to USD 6.1 billion by 2035, at a CAGR of 5.4%. This growth is driven by increasing concerns about foodborne illnesses, stricter regulations, and the adoption of automated sanitation systems. Key regions like Asia Pacific, North America, and Europe are contributing significantly to this expansion, with major companies like Ecolab, Diversey, 3M, and BASF leading the competitive landscape.

feed.title: Raymond James raises ConocoPhillips price target to $145 on oil strip
feed.summary: Raymond James has increased its price target for ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) to $145 from $118, maintaining an Outperform rating due to a higher oil strip. The firm's updated estimates reflect current market conditions and production impacts from the Qatar disruption, projecting earnings of $1.70 per share for Q1 2026. ConocoPhillips is also reportedly considering selling assets in the Permian Basin and has received other analyst updates, including a $157 price target from Piper Sandler and new coverage from Truist Securities.

feed.title: KBR Secures $200 Million Contract to Enhance Aviation Safety and Modernize Transportation Networks
feed.summary: KBR has secured a five-year, $200 million recompete contract with the U.S. Department of Transportation's Volpe Center to enhance aviation safety and modernize transportation networks. The company will leverage its AI-driven solutions to provide IT and systems engineering capabilities, focusing on intelligent transportation systems, navigation technologies, and safety oversight processes. This contract underscores KBR's long-standing partnership with the Volpe Center and its role in improving U.S. transportation infrastructure.
